title: War spurs EU plan for electricity tax cuts, faster shift from fossil fuels, draft shows
description: By Kate Abnett BRUSSELS, April 14 (Reuters) - The European Union plans to reduce electricity taxes and expand clean technologies faster to cut consumers' exposure to soaring oil and gas prices, as part of its plan to offsetthe Iran war's energy impact, a draft document showed. Europe's ​heavy reliance on oil and gas imports has left it exposed to spiralling prices ⁠since the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global oil and gas shipping route, was effectively closed and Tehran ​started attacking energy infrastructure in the Middle East.
